content.query:
-- Cell 8: Create External Tables
-- Gold Layer — Synapse Serverless

-- this script creates:
-- Create VIEWS using OPENROWSET
-- It is the recommended pattern for synapse Serverless + Parquet files

USE stock_gold;

GO
-- fact_stock_prices view
IF EXISTS (
    SELECT * FROM sys.views
    WHERE name = 'fact_stock_prices'
)
DROP VIEW fact_stock_prices;
GO
CREATE VIEW fact_stock_prices AS
SELECT *
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://stockdata@stockdatalake237.dfs.core.windows.net/gold/fact_stock_prices/',
    FORMAT = 'PARQUET'
) AS result;

GO

-- dim_dates view
IF EXISTS (
    SELECT * FROM sys.views
    WHERE name = 'dim_dates'
)
DROP VIEW dim_dates;
GO
CREATE VIEW dim_dates AS
SELECT *
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://stockdata@stockdatalake237.dfs.core.windows.net/gold/dim_dates/',
    FORMAT = 'PARQUET'
) AS result;
GO

-- dim_tickers view
IF EXISTS (
    SELECT * FROM sys.views
    WHERE name = 'dim_tickers'
)
DROP VIEW dim_tickers;
GO
CREATE VIEW dim_tickers AS
SELECT *
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'abfss://stockdata@stockdatalake237.dfs.core.windows.net/gold/dim_tickers/',
    FORMAT = 'PARQUET'
) AS result;

source:
#  Test Connection to Azure SQL

# Azure SQL connection details

server   = "stocks-sql-server.database.windows.net"
database = "stocks-db"
username = "sqladmin"
password = "Azuresql@237"  

# Build the JDBC URL
# Format: jdbc:sqlserver://server:port;database=name
jdbc_url = f"jdbc:sqlserver://{server}:1433;database={database}"

# Connection properties dictionary
# Tells Spark HOW to authenticate
connection_properties = {
    "user"    : username,
    "password": password,
    "driver"  : "com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerDriver"
}

print(" Connection details configured!")
print(f"   Server  : {server}")
print(f"   Database: {database}")
print(f"   JDBC URL: {jdbc_url}")

# Read raw_stocks from Azure SQL
# into a PySpark DataFrame


print("Reading raw_stock from Azure SQL....")

df_stocks = spark.read.jdbc(
    url = jdbc_url,
    table = "raw_stocks",
    properties = connection_properties
)
df_stocks= df_stocks.select(
    "ticker",
    "trade_date",
    "open_price",
    "high_price",
    "low_price",
    "close_price",
    "volume",
    "ingested_at"   
)

# .cache() tells Spark to keep this DataFrame in memory
# so we don't re-read from Azure SQL every time we use it
df_stocks.cache()

#counting the number of rows read
row_count = df_stocks.count()
print(f" Successfully read raw_stocks!")
print(f" Total rows: {row_count}")

# printSchema() shows the structure of the DataFrame
print("\nSchema (columns and data types):")
df_stocks.printSchema()

#  Read stock_metadata from Azure SQL
# This is our reference/dimension table
# Contains company info for each ticker



print("Reading stock_metadata from Azure SQL...")

df_metadata = spark.read.jdbc(
    url        = jdbc_url,
    table      = "stock_metadata",
    properties = connection_properties
)

df_metadata.cache()

row_count = df_metadata.count()
print(f" Successfully read stock_metadata!")
print(f"  Total rows: {row_count}")

print("\nSchema:")
df_metadata.printSchema()

print("\nAll rows (only 5 tickers):")
df_metadata.show(5)

# Read technical_indicators from Azure SQl


print("Reading technical_indicators from Azure SQL...")

df_indicators = spark.read.jdbc(
    url        = jdbc_url,
    table      = "technical_indicators",
    properties = connection_properties
)

df_indicators.cache()

row_count = df_indicators.count()
print(f" Successfully read technical_indicators!")
print(f" Total rows: {row_count}")

print("\nSchema:")
df_indicators.printSchema()

# Show first 5 rows to preview the data
print("\nFirst 5 rows:")
df_indicators.show(5)

#  Calculate True MACD

# EMA Formula:
# EMA = prices.ewm(alpha=k, adjust=False).mean()
# ewm = Exponentially Weighted Mean
# alpha = smoothing factor k
# adjust=False = recursive formula (not weighted sum)


from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, DateType, DoubleType
from pyspark.sql.functions import col
import pandas as pd

# Smoothing factors
k_12 = 2.0 / (12 + 1)
k_26 = 2.0 / (26 + 1)
k_9  = 2.0 / (9  + 1)

# Output schema
macd_schema = StructType([
    StructField("ticker",      StringType(), True),
    StructField("trade_date",  DateType(),   True),
    StructField("close_price", DoubleType(), True),
    StructField("macd_line",   DoubleType(), True),
    StructField("macd_signal", DoubleType(), True),
    StructField("macd_hist",   DoubleType(), True),
])

def calculate_macd(ticker_df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    # Sort oldest to newest — critical for EMA
    ticker_df = ticker_df.sort_values("trade_date")
    
    # Calculate EMA 12 and EMA 26 using pandas
    ema_12 = ticker_df["close_price"].ewm(alpha=k_12, adjust=False).mean()
    ema_26 = ticker_df["close_price"].ewm(alpha=k_26, adjust=False).mean()
    
    # MACD line — use pandas .round() NOT pyspark round()
    ticker_df["macd_line"]   = (ema_12 - ema_26).round(4)
    
    # Signal line
    ticker_df["macd_signal"] = ticker_df["macd_line"].ewm(alpha=k_9, adjust=False).mean().round(4)
    
    # Histogram
    ticker_df["macd_hist"]   = (
        ticker_df["macd_line"] - ticker_df["macd_signal"]
    ).round(4)
    
    return ticker_df[[
        "ticker", "trade_date", "close_price",
        "macd_line", "macd_signal", "macd_hist"
    ]]

# Extract close prices
df_prices = df_stocks.select(
    col("ticker"),
    col("trade_date"),
    col("close_price").cast("double")
)

# Apply to all tickers
df_macd_complete = df_prices\
    .groupBy("ticker")\
    .applyInPandas(calculate_macd, schema=macd_schema)

print(f" MACD calculated!")
print(f" Total rows: {df_macd_complete.count()}")

print("\nAAPL MACD — last 5 days:")
df_macd_complete\
    .filter(col("ticker") == "MSFT")\
    .orderBy(col("trade_date").desc())\
    .show(50)

#Calculate Bollinger Bands

# BB Middle = SMA 20 (already in df_indicators)
# BB Upper  = SMA 20 + (2 × 20-day stddev)
# BB Lower  = SMA 20 - (2 × 20-day stddev)
#
# stddev = standard deviation of close price
# over last 20 days — measures volatility

from pyspark.sql.functions import stddev, col, round
from pyspark.sql import Window


# Window — last 20 days per ticker
window_20=Window.partitionBy("ticker").orderBy("trade_date").rowsBetween(-19, 0)

# Calculate 20-day rolling standard deviation
df_with_stddev = df_stocks.select(
    "ticker",
    "trade_date",
    "close_price"
).withColumn(
    "stddev_20",
    stddev(col("close_price")).over(window_20)
)

print("standard diviation calculated")

# Join with indicators to get SMA_20
df_bb = df_with_stddev.join(
    df_indicators.select("ticker", "trade_date","sma_20"),
    on=["ticker","trade_date"],
    how="left"
)

df_bollinger = df_bb.withColumn(
    "bb_middle",
    round(col("sma_20"),4)
).withColumn(
    "bb_upper",
    round(col("sma_20")+(2*col("stddev_20")),4)
).withColumn(
    "bb_lower",
    round(col("sma_20")-(2*col("stddev_20")),4)
).select(
    "ticker",
    "trade_date",
    "bb_upper",
    "bb_middle",
    "bb_lower"
)

print("Bollinger Bands calculated!")

#preview
print("\n Bollinger bands - AAPL last 5 days:")
df_bollinger\
    .filter(col("ticker")=="AAPL")\
    .orderBy(col("trade_date").desc())\
    .show(5)

df_ind_slim = df_indicators.select(
    "ticker",
    "trade_date",
    "sma_20",
    "ema_20",
    "rsi_14"
)

print("indicators column retrived!\n")

df2_stock_slim = df_stocks.select(
    "ticker",
    "trade_date",
    "open_price",
    "high_price",
    "low_price",
    "volume",
    "ingested_at"
)

print("row_stocks column retrived!")

df_final=df_macd_complete\
.join(df_ind_slim, on=["ticker","trade_date"], how="left")\
.join(df2_stock_slim, on=["ticker","trade_date"], how="left")\
.join(df_bollinger, on=["ticker", "trade_date"], how="left")

print("all dataframes joined!")
print(f"total rows:{df_final.count()} ")
print(f"total columns:{len(df_final.columns)}")

#preview
print(" first 5 rows of the final dataframe")
df_final.show(5)

print("\nAll columns in df_final:")
print(df_final.columns)

df_metadata_slim = df_metadata.select(
    "ticker",
    "company_name",
    "sector",
    "industry",
    "exchange"
)

df_final= df_final.join(df_metadata_slim, on=["ticker"], how="left")

print(f"total columns: {len(df_final.columns)}")
df_final.show(5)

# Write Gold Layer to Data Lake
# We write 3 tables as Parquet files:
# 1. dim_tickers    : company reference data
# 2. dim_dates      : date dimension
# 3. fact_stock_prices : all analytics data




from pyspark.sql.functions import (
    col, year, month, dayofweek, weekofyear, date_format,
    when, lit, monotonically_increasing_id, quarter,dayofmonth,
    current_timestamp
)
from pyspark.sql.types import IntegerType

storage_account= "stockdatalake237"
container= "stockdata"

# Full path format for PySpark
base_path="abfss://stockdata@stockdatalake237.dfs.core.windows.net/"

gold_path= base_path+"gold/"

print(f" Storage path configured:")
print(f" Base : {base_path}")
print(f" Gold : {gold_path}")

#dim_tickers

df_dim_tickers= df_metadata.select(
    "ticker",
    "company_name",
    "exchange",
    "currency",
    "country",
    "sector",
    "industry"
).withColumn(
    "ticker_key",
    monotonically_increasing_id().cast(IntegerType()) + 1
).select(
    "ticker_key",
    "ticker",
    "company_name",
    "exchange",
    "currency",
    "country",
    "sector",
    "industry"
)

#The data lake is now written as parquet
df_dim_tickers.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(gold_path + "dim_tickers/")

print(f"   dim_tickers written!")
print(f"   Rows: {df_dim_tickers.count()}")
print(f"   Path: {gold_path}dim_tickers/")


# Generate and Write dim_dates

df_dates_raw = df_final\
    .select("trade_date")\
    .distinct()\
    .orderBy("trade_date")

# Build dim_dates with all Kimball attributes
df_dim_dates = df_dates_raw.withColumn(
    # date_key = integer version of date
    # 2026-03-20 : 20260320
    # Useful for fast integer joins
    "date_key",
    (
        year(col("trade_date")) * 10000 +
        month(col("trade_date")) * 100 +
        dayofmonth(col("trade_date")) #.cast("string").substr(-2, 2).cast(IntegerType())
    ).cast(IntegerType())
).withColumn(
    "year",
    year(col("trade_date"))
).withColumn(
    "month",
    month(col("trade_date"))
).withColumn(
    "month_name",
    date_format(col("trade_date"), "MMMM") # Full month name e.g. "March"
).withColumn(
    # Quarter e.g. "Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"
    "quarter",
    when(month(col("trade_date")).between(1, 3),  "Q1")
    .when(month(col("trade_date")).between(4, 6),  "Q2")
    .when(month(col("trade_date")).between(7, 9),  "Q3")
    .otherwise("Q4")
).withColumn(
    # Day of week name
    "day_of_week",
    date_format(col("trade_date"), "EEEE")
).withColumn(
    # Week number of the year
    "week_number",
    weekofyear(col("trade_date"))
).withColumn(
    # Is this a weekend? (1=Sunday, 7=Saturday in Spark)
    "is_weekend",
    when(dayofweek(col("trade_date")).isin(1, 7), True)
    .otherwise(False)
).withColumn(
    # is_holiday : False for now
    # a holiday calendar will be loaded in production
    "is_holiday",
    lit(False)
)

# Write to Data Lake
df_dim_dates.write\
    .mode("overwrite")\
    .parquet(gold_path + "dim_dates/")

print(f"  dim_dates written!")
print(f"  Rows: {df_dim_dates.count()}")
print(f"  Path: {gold_path}dim_dates/")

# Preview dim_dates
print("\n  Sample dim_dates rows:")
df_dim_dates.show(3)


# Write fact_stock_prices
#we are joining the df_final with dim_dates and din_tickers so as to get surrogat keys

print("\n[3/3] Writing fact_stock_prices...")

# Join to get ticker_key from dim_tickers
df_fact = df_final.join(
    df_dim_tickers.select("ticker", "ticker_key"),
    on="ticker",
    how="left"
).join(
    df_dim_dates.select("trade_date", "date_key"),
    on="trade_date",
    how="left"
)

# Add surrogate key for fact table
df_fact = df_fact.withColumn(
    "stock_key",
    monotonically_increasing_id().cast(IntegerType()) + 1
)

df_fact_final = df_fact.select(
    "stock_key",    # surrogate PK
    "date_key",     # FK to dim_dates
    "ticker_key",   # FK to dim_tickers
    "ticker",       
    "trade_date",   
    "open_price",
    "high_price",
    "low_price",
    "close_price",
    "volume",
    "sma_20",
    "ema_20",
    "rsi_14",
    "macd_line",
    "macd_signal",
    "macd_hist",
    "bb_upper",
    "bb_middle",
    "bb_lower",
    "ingested_at"
).withColumn(
    "loaded_at",
    current_timestamp()
)

df_fact_final.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(gold_path + "fact_stock_prices/")

print(f"     fact_stock_prices written!")
print(f"     Rows: {df_fact_final.count()}")
print(f"     Path: {gold_path}fact_stock_prices/")

print("\n" + "=" * 55)
print("GOLD LAYER WRITE COMPLETE")
print("=" * 55)
print(f"   dim_tickers      : {df_dim_tickers.count()} rows")
print(f"   dim_dates        : {df_dim_dates.count()} rows")
print(f"   fact_stock_prices : {df_fact_final.count()} rows")
print(f"\n Location: {gold_path}")
print("\n  Gold layer successfully written to Data Lake!")

content.query:
USE stock_gold;
GO

-- Query 1: Current RSI for all tickers
-- Which stocks are oversold or overbought?

SELECT
    ticker,
    trade_date,
    close_price,
    rsi_14,
    CASE
        WHEN rsi_14 < 30 THEN 'OVERSOLD - Potential BUY'
        WHEN rsi_14 > 70 THEN 'OVERBOUGHT - Potential SELL'
        ELSE 'NEUTRAL'
    END AS rsi_signal
FROM fact_stock_prices
WHERE trade_date = (
    SELECT MAX(trade_date)
    FROM fact_stock_prices
)
ORDER BY rsi_14;


-- Query 2: Average volume by sector

SELECT
    t.sector,
    COUNT(DISTINCT f.ticker) AS num_stocks,
    AVG(f.volume)            AS avg_volume,
    AVG(f.close_price)       AS avg_price
FROM fact_stock_prices f
JOIN dim_tickers t ON f.ticker_key = t.ticker_key
GROUP BY t.sector
ORDER BY avg_volume DESC;


-- Query 3: MACD trend signals

SELECT
    ticker,
    trade_date,
    close_price,
    macd_line,
    macd_signal,
    CASE
        WHEN macd_line > macd_signal THEN 'BULLISH'
        WHEN macd_line < macd_signal THEN 'BEARISH'
        ELSE 'NEUTRAL'
    END AS macd_trend
FROM fact_stock_prices
WHERE trade_date = (
    SELECT MAX(trade_date)
    FROM fact_stock_prices
)
ORDER BY ticker;


-- Query 4: Monthly performance by ticker

SELECT
    f.ticker,
    d.month_name,
    d.year,
    AVG(f.close_price) AS avg_close,
    MAX(f.high_price)  AS month_high,
    MIN(f.low_price)   AS month_low,
    SUM(f.volume)      AS total_volume
FROM fact_stock_prices f
JOIN dim_dates d ON f.date_key = d.date_key
GROUP BY f.ticker, d.month_name, d.year
ORDER BY f.ticker, d.year, MIN(d.trade_date);
